children.heading: Creation of Trust Fund
children.label: a Creation of Trust Fund
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States a trust fund to be known as the “Highway Trust Fund”, consisting of such amounts as may be appropriated or credited to the Highway Trust Fund as provided in this section or section 9602(b).

children.heading: Transfer to Highway Trust Fund of amounts equivalent to certain taxes and penalties
children.label: b Transfer to Highway Trust Fund of amounts equivalent to certain taxes and penalties
A
children.text: section 4041 (relating to taxes on diesel fuels and special motor fuels),

children.label: B
children.text: section 4051 (relating to retail tax on heavy trucks and trailers),

children.label: C
children.text: section 4071 (relating to tax on tires),

children.label: D
children.text: section 4081 (relating to tax on gasoline, diesel fuel, and kerosene), and

children.label: E
children.text: section 4481 (relating to tax on use of certain vehicles).

children.heading: Liabilities incurred beforeOctober 1, 2028
children.label: 2 Liabilities incurred beforeOctober 1, 2028
children.text: There are hereby appropriated to the Highway Trust Fund amounts equivalent to the taxes which are received in the Treasury after , and before , and which are attributable to liability for tax incurred before , under the provisions described in paragraph (1).September 30, 2028July 1, 2029October 1, 2028

children.heading: Certain taxes not transferred to Highway Trust Fund
children.label: 4 Certain taxes not transferred to Highway Trust Fund
A
children.text: section 4041(d),

children.label: B
children.text: section 4081 to the extent attributable to the rate specified in section 4081(a)(2)(B),

children.label: C
children.text: section 4041 or 4081 to the extent attributable to fuel used in a train, or

children.label: i
children.text: 11.5 cents per gallon with respect to taxes imposed before ,October 1, 2001

children.label: ii
children.text: 13 cents per gallon with respect to taxes imposed after , and before , andSeptember 30, 2001October 1, 2003

children.label: iii
children.text: 13.5 cents per gallon with respect to taxes imposed after , and before .September 30, 2003October 1, 2005

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: There are hereby appropriated to the Highway Trust Fund amounts equivalent to the penalties paid under sections 6715, 6715A, 6717, 6718, 6719, 6720A, 6725, 7232, and 7272 (but only with regard to penalties under such section related to failure to register under section 4101).

children.heading: Penalties related to motor vehicle safety
children.label: B Penalties related to motor vehicle safety

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: There are hereby appropriated to the Highway Trust Fund amounts equivalent to covered motor vehicle safety penalty collections.

children.heading: Covered motor vehicle safety penalty collections
children.label: ii Covered motor vehicle safety penalty collections
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “covered motor vehicle safety penalty collections” means any amount collected in connection with a civil penalty under , United States Code, reduced by any award authorized by the Secretary of Transportation to be paid to any person in connection with information provided by such person related to a violation of chapter 301 of such title which is a predicate to such civil penalty.section 30165 of title 49

children.heading: Limitation on transfers to Highway Trust Fund
children.label: 6 Limitation on transfers to Highway Trust Fund
i
children.text: any provision of law which is not contained or referenced in this title or in a revenue Act, and

children.label: ii
children.text: whether such provision of law is a subsequently enacted provision or directly or indirectly seeks to waive the application of this paragraph.

children.heading: Exception for prior obligations
children.label: B Exception for prior obligations
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any expenditure to liquidate any contract entered into (or for any amount otherwise obligated) before , in accordance with the provisions of this section.October 1, 2026

children.heading: Expenditures from Highway Trust Fund
children.label: c Expenditures from Highway Trust Fund

children.heading: Federal-aid highway program
children.label: 1 Federal-aid highway program
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (e), amounts in the Highway Trust Fund shall be available, as provided by appropriation Acts, for making expenditures before , to meet those obligations of the United States heretofore or hereafter incurred which are authorized to be paid out of the Highway Trust Fund under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act or any other provision of law which was referred to in this paragraph before the date of the enactment of such Act (as such Act and provisions of law are in effect on the date of the enactment of such Act).October 1, 2026

children.heading: Floor stocks refunds
children.label: 2 Floor stocks refunds
children.text: The Secretary shall pay from time to time from the Highway Trust Fund into the general fund of the Treasury amounts equivalent to the floor stocks refunds made before , under section 6412(a). The amounts payable from the Highway Trust Fund under the preceding sentence shall be determined by taking into account only the portion of the taxes which are deposited into the Highway Trust Fund.July 1, 2029

children.heading: Transfers from the Trust Fund for motorboat fuel taxes
children.label: 3 Transfers from the Trust Fund for motorboat fuel taxes

children.heading: Transfer to Land and Water Conservation Fund
children.label: A Transfer to Land and Water Conservation Fund

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall pay from time to time from the Highway Trust Fund into the land and water conservation fund provided for in chapter 2003 of title 54 amounts (as determined by the Secretary) equivalent to the motorboat fuel taxes received on or after , and before .October 1, 2005October 1, 2028

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: The aggregate amount transferred under this subparagraph during any fiscal year shall not exceed $1,000,000.

children.heading: Excess funds transferred to Sport Fish Restoration and Boating Trust Fund
children.label: B Excess funds transferred to Sport Fish Restoration and Boating Trust Fund
i
children.text: which are attributable to motorboat fuel taxes, and

children.label: ii
children.text: which are not transferred from the Highway Trust Fund under subparagraph (A),

children.heading: Motorboat fuel taxes
children.label: C Motorboat fuel taxes
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “motorboat fuel taxes” means the taxes under section 4041(a)(2) with respect to special motor fuels used as fuel in motorboats and under section 4081 with respect to gasoline used as fuel in motorboats, but only to the extent such taxes are deposited into the Highway Trust Fund.

children.heading: Determination
children.label: D Determination
children.text: The amount of payments made under this paragraph after shall be determined by the Secretary in accordance with the methodology described in the Treasury Department’s Report to Congress of June 1986 entitled “Gasoline Excise Tax Revenues Attributable to Fuel Used in Recreational Motorboats.”October 1, 1986

children.heading: Transfers from the Trust Fund for small-engine fuel taxes
children.label: 4 Transfers from the Trust Fund for small-engine fuel taxes

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall pay from time to time from the Highway Trust Fund into the Sport Fish Restoration and Boating Trust Fund amounts (as determined by him) equivalent to the small-engine fuel taxes received on or after , and before .December 1, 1990October 1, 2028

children.heading: Small-engine fuel taxes
children.label: B Small-engine fuel taxes
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “small-engine fuel taxes” means the taxes under section 4081 with respect to gasoline used as a fuel in the nonbusiness use of small-engine outdoor power equipment, but only to the extent such taxes are deposited into the Highway Trust Fund.

children.heading: Transfers from the Trust Fund for certain aviation fuel taxes
children.label: 5 Transfers from the Trust Fund for certain aviation fuel taxes
A
children.text: 4.3 cents per gallon of kerosene subject to section 6427()(4)(A) with respect to which a payment has been made by the Secretary under section 6427(), andll

children.label: B
children.text: 21.8 cents per gallon of kerosene subject to section 6427()(4)(B) with respect to which a payment has been made by the Secretary under section 6427().ll

children.label: 1 Estimates of unfunded highway authorizations and net highway receipts
A
children.text: the amount which would (but for this subsection) be the unfunded highway authorizations at the close of the next fiscal year, and

children.label: B
children.text: the net highway receipts for the 48-month period beginning at the close of such fiscal year.

children.label: 2 Procedure where there is excess unfunded highway authorizations
A
children.text: he shall so advise the Secretary of Transportation, and

children.label: 3 Adjustment of apportionments where unfunded authorizations exceed 4 years’ receipts
i
children.text: the excess referred to in paragraph (2)(B), is of

children.heading: Adjustment of apportionments
children.label: B Adjustment of apportionments
children.text: If the Secretary of Transportation determines a percentage under subparagraph (A) for purposes of any apportionment, notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary of Transportation shall apportion to the States (in lieu of the amount which, but for the provisions of this subsection, would be so apportioned) the amount obtained by reducing the amount authorized to be so apportioned by such percentage.

children.heading: Apportionment of amounts previously withheld from apportionment
children.label: 4 Apportionment of amounts previously withheld from apportionment
children.text: If, after funds have been withheld from apportionment under paragraph (3)(B), the Secretary of the Treasury determines that the amount described in paragraph (1)(A) does not exceed the amount described in paragraph (1)(B) or that the excess described in paragraph (1)(B) is less than the amount previously determined, he shall so advise the Secretary of Transportation. The Secretary of Transportation shall apportion to the States such portion of the funds so withheld from apportionment as the Secretary of the Treasury has advised him may be so apportioned without causing the amount described in paragraph (1)(A) to exceed the amount described in paragraph (1)(B). Any funds apportioned pursuant to the preceding sentence shall remain available for the period for which they would be available if such apportionment took effect with the fiscal year in which they are apportioned pursuant to the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: 7 Reports
children.text: Any estimate under paragraph (1) and any determination under paragraph (2) shall be reported by the Secretary of the Treasury to the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives, the Committee on Finance of the Senate, the Committees on the Budget of both Houses, the Committee on Public Works and Transportation of the House of Representatives, and the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate.

children.heading: Establishment of Mass Transit Account
children.label: e Establishment of Mass Transit Account

children.heading: Creation of account
children.label: 1 Creation of account
children.text: There is established in the Highway Trust Fund a separate account to be known as the “Mass Transit Account” consisting of such amounts as may be transferred or credited to the Mass Transit Account as provided in this section or section 9602(b).

children.heading: Expenditures from Account
children.label: 3 Expenditures from Account
children.text: Amounts in the Mass Transit Account shall be available, as provided by appropriation Acts, for making capital or capital related expenditures (including capital expenditures for new projects) before , in accordance with the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act or any other provision of law which was referred to in this paragraph before the date of the enactment of such Act (as such Act and provisions of law are in effect on the date of the enactment of such Act).October 1, 2026

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 4 Limitation
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of subsection (d) shall apply to the Mass Transit Account.

children.heading: Portion of certain transfers to be made from account
children.label: 5 Portion of certain transfers to be made from account

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Transfers under paragraphs (2) and (3) of subsection (c) shall be borne by the Highway Account and the Mass Transit Account in proportion to the respective revenues transferred under this section to the Highway Account (after the application of paragraph (2)) and the Mass Transit Account.

children.heading: Highway Account
children.label: B Highway Account
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “Highway Account” means the portion of the Highway Trust Fund which is not the Mass Transit Account.
